message:
feat(core,state): Extend RibosomeTrafficState with history and add pressure overlay

Core simulation type extensions (packages/core/src/simulation.ts):
- Add densityHistory: number[] to RibosomeTrafficState
  - Tracks rolling history of active ribosome counts per tick
  - Enables visualization of traffic density trends over time
  - Used for sparkline rendering in RibosomeTrafficView

- Add productionHistory: number[] to RibosomeTrafficState
  - Tracks cumulative protein output over simulation time
  - Delta between consecutive values gives production rate
  - Enables "proteins per step" sparkline for throughput analysis

State management (packages/state/src/store.ts):
- Add 'pressure' to OverlayId union type
  - New overlay for PackagingPressureOverlay component
  - Triggered by V key (intermediate+ experience level)
  - Visualizes DNA packaging motor pressure based on scroll position

The history arrays support time-series visualization without maintaining
separate state - the simulation step function appends to the arrays and
trims to keep bounded memory (200 entries max per the registry impl).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-5b5","title":"Foundation: Advanced UI State Management","description":"## Advanced UI State Management

Zustand state structure supporting overlay/modal/simulation/command systems.

### AdvancedUIState Interface
```typescript
interface AdvancedUIState {
  // Overlay system
  activeOverlays: Set<OverlayId>;
  overlayConfigs: Map<OverlayId, OverlayConfig>;

  // Modal system
  activeModal: ModalId | null;
  modalHistory: ModalId[];  // For back navigation

  // Simulation state
  activeSimulation: SimulationId | null;
  simulationState: SimState | null;
  simulationSpeed: number;
  simulationPaused: boolean;

  // Command palette
  commandPaletteOpen: boolean;
  commandSearch: string;
  commandResults: CommandMatch[];
  selectedCommandIndex: number;

  // Computed data cache
  analysisCache: Map<string, AnalysisResult>;
}
```

### Key Principle
Precompute on phage selection, not on feature activation. When user selects a phage, background-compute: GC skew, complexity, bendability, promoter predictions. Makes overlays feel instant when toggled.

Depends on: All 4 foundation systems (Overlay, Menu, Command Palette, Simulation)","notes":"Advanced UI state implemented: overlay stack, menu/simulation hubs, command palette state, precompute overlays on phage load, comparison + simulation state tracked in store","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:36.359231-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:23:18.530353-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T13:23:18.530363-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-5b5","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-2g5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:36.360877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-5b5","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:36.361949-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-5b5","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-8lh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:36.362786-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-5b5","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:36.363707-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"phage_explorer-5jr","title":"Advanced: HGT Provenance Tracer","description":"Infer donor clades for genomic islands via GC/codon atypicality + k-mer taxonomic assignment (Mash/MinHash). Generate passport stamps per island with donor lineage, confidence, hallmark genes. Genome bar with colored islands; stamp shows donor pie and GC/codon plots. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## HORIZONTAL GENE TRANSFER PROVENANCE TRACER

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
For each genomic island, infer donor clades using GC/codon atypicality, best-hit taxonomy, and mini phylo placement; generate 'passport stamps' per island.

### How to Build

**Island Detection:**
- GC/codon Z-scores vs genome background
- Dinucleotide bias (ρ-statistic)
- Sliding window in TS or Rust+WASM
- Threshold: |Z| > 2 or 3 for significant deviation

**Donor Inference:**
- Fast k-mer taxonomic assignment (Mash/MinHash) vs reference panel
- Optional short-tree placement:
  - IQ-TREE-lite binding
  - Neighbor-joining (Rust+WASM) for speed

**Passport Stamps Store:**
- Donor lineage (phylum, genus, species)
- Confidence score
- Hallmark genes (AMGs, defense, anti-defense)
- GC/codon deviation metrics

### TUI Features
- Genome bar with colored islands (by donor clade)
- Opening a stamp shows:
  - Donor pie chart
  - GC/codon deviation plots
  - Top donor reference matches
- Island list with confidence and recency estimate
- Jump to island on click

### Why It's Good
- Explains where novel modules came from
- Estimates transfer recency
- Guides risk assessment and host-range hypotheses
- Shows horizontal gene flow patterns

### Implementation Stack
- TS for GC/codon statistics
- Rust+WASM for MinHash and small trees
- SQLite cache of islands and stamps
- Ink UI with colored genome bar

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:17:42.024781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:26:40.652963-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-5jr","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:20:52.376411-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"phage_explorer-5jr","title":"Advanced: HGT Provenance Tracer","description":"Infer donor clades for genomic islands via GC/codon atypicality + k-mer taxonomic assignment (Mash/MinHash). Generate passport stamps per island with donor lineage, confidence, hallmark genes. Genome bar with colored islands; stamp shows donor pie and GC/codon plots. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## HORIZONTAL GENE TRANSFER PROVENANCE TRACER

**Full TypeScript Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Create forensic 'passport stamps' for genomic islands identifying donor clades, transfer timing, and functional annotation.

### Mathematical Foundations

**Island Detection via Z-Score:**
```
GC_z(w) = (GC_w - μ_genome) / σ_genome

Flag window if |GC_z| > 2.0
```

**Dinucleotide Relative Abundance:**
```
ρ(XY) = f(XY) / (f(X) × f(Y))
Z_dinuc = Σ|ρ_island(XY) - ρ_genome(XY)| / 16
```

**MinHash Jaccard Similarity:**
```
J(A, B) ≈ |MinHash(A) ∩ MinHash(B)| / |MinHash(A) ∪ MinHash(B)|
Containment: C(A, B) = |MinHash(A) ∩ MinHash(B)| / |MinHash(A)|
```

**Amelioration Time Estimate:**
```
t_amelioration ∝ -ln(|CAI_island - CAI_genome|) / μ_mutation
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `GenomicIsland` interface (start, end, GC, z-scores, genes, hallmarks)
- `DonorCandidate` with taxon, similarity, confidence, evidence type
- `PassportStamp` with island, donor, amelioration, mechanism
- `HGTAnalysis` full analysis result
- `computeGCContent()`, `computeDinucleotideFrequencies()`
- `computeDinucleotideOddsRatio()`, `dinucleotideZScore()`
- `detectIslands()` sliding window with merge
- `createIsland()` with hallmark gene detection
- `murmurhash3()` hash function
- `computeMinHash()` k-mer sketching
- `jaccardFromMinHash()` similarity calculation
- `inferDonors()` reference database query
- `estimateAmelioration()` recent/intermediate/ancient
- `inferTransferMechanism()` lysogeny/transduction/conjugation
- `analyzeHGTProvenance()` main pipeline
- `renderIslandGenomeBar()` ASCII visualization
- `renderPassportStamp()` detailed stamp view

### TUI Features
- Genome bar with colored islands (by amelioration: recent/intermediate/ancient)
- 'Passport stamp' popup per island showing:
  - Location and GC deviation
  - Top donor with similarity
  - Donor distribution bars
  - GC/codon profile sparkline
  - Hallmark genes
  - Transfer mechanism
  - Amelioration estimate
- Summary: total islands, recent/ancient counts, dominant donors, foreign DNA %
- [←/→] Navigate, [D] Donor details, [P] Phylo tree

### Why It's Good
- Forensic tracing of foreign DNA origins
- Transfer timing estimation via amelioration
- Connects to host range and safety assessment
- 'Passport stamp' metaphor makes analysis accessible

 {"id":"phage_explorer-6sn","title":"Advanced: Recombination Hotspot Radar","description":"Advanced: Detect mosaic ancestry along the genome using change-point detection on k-mer/embedding profiles and local phylogenies. Reveals chimeric origins and recent recombination donors.

**Build Plan:**
- Segment with ruptures-like change-point (Rust + ndarray) on embedding/k-mer vectors
- For each segment, compute nearest clade via sketch (Mash/MinHash) and mini-tree
- Cache segment→clade calls in SQLite
- WASM for change-point + sketching; small phylo in TS for speed

**Recombination Detection Methods:**
- K-mer profile discontinuities
- Embedding vector jumps
- Local phylogeny incongruence
- Change-point significance testing

**TUI Visualization:**
- Genome heat strip with colors per donor clade
- Cursor shows donor list and support
- Press 'r' to highlight high-confidence recombination breakpoints
- Timeline view showing recombination history

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Cool Factor: 9/10.","notes":"## RECOMBINATION & MOSAICISM RADAR (SLIDING-WINDOW PHYLO)

### How to Build
- **Windows**: Slice genome; compute k-mer Jaccard/Mash distances to a panel; optional fast NJ per window (Rust+WASM)
- **Breakpoint calling**: Change-point detection (PELT/Bayesian) on similarity traces; store segments with top donors
- **Donor hints**: Track which reference best matches each window; summarize donor composition

### TUI Visualization
Circular radar with colored arcs; move cursor to see donor and similarity; press 'b' to jump to breakpoints; sparkline of similarity underneath.

- packages/core/src/analysis/recombination-radar.ts
- packages/tui/src/components/MosaicRadarView.tsx","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:19:11.092247-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:06:34.317841-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-6sn","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:19:11.093779-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-7am","title":"Advanced: Burst Kinetics & Latency Inference","description":"Infer adsorption rate, latent period, and burst size from OD/plaque time-series using SIR-like infection ODE/SDE. MAP/bootstrapped fits with credible intervals. Map parameters back to holin/endolysin/antiholin loci. Live fit plot, toggle deterministic/stochastic, residuals pane. Bridges wet-lab curves to genome-linked parameters. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## BURST KINETICS & LATENCY INFERENCE FROM GROWTH CURVES

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Mathematical Foundations

**Classic Infection ODE Model:**
```
dB/dt = μB - k·B·P            # Bacteria: growth - infection
dI/dt = k·B·P - I/L           # Infected: new infections - lysis
dP/dt = (b/L)·I - k·B·P - δP  # Phages: burst - adsorption - decay
```

**Age-Structured Infected Population:**
```
∂i(t,a)/∂t + ∂i(t,a)/∂a = -γ(a)·i(t,a)
Boundary: i(t,0) = k·B(t)·P(t)
```

**Likelihood Function for Parameter Inference:**
```
L(θ|D) = ∏ᵢ N(yᵢ | f(tᵢ,θ), σ²)
θ = {k, L, b, μ, δ, B₀, P₀}
```

**Stochastic Gillespie Model Propensities:**
```
a₁ = μ·B       # Bacterial division
a₂ = k·B·P     # Adsorption
a₃ = (1/L)·I   # Lysis (releases b phages)
a₄ = δ·P       # Phage decay
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `InfectionParameters` interface (k, L, b, μ, δ, B₀, P₀)
- `rungeKutta4()` 4th-order ODE solver
- `infectionODE()` system definition
- `simulateInfection()` trajectory generation
- `logLikelihood()` for parameter estimation
- `nelderMead()` optimization
- `identifyLysisCassette()` gene linking
- `fitInfectionModel()` main inference function
- `linkKineticsToGenome()` cassette→kinetics mapping

### TUI Features
- Growth Curve Fit with 95% CI shading
- Inferred Parameters panel (k, L, b with confidence intervals)
- Lysis Cassette visualization (holin, antiholin, endolysin, spanin)
- Latency prediction match indicator
- R² and AIC model fit statistics
- [D]ata toggle, [M]ode ODE/Gillespie, [B]ootstrap, [J]ump to gene

### Implementation Stack
- **ODE Solver**: Rust + WASM for RK4 with adaptive stepping
- **Optimization**: Nelder-Mead in TypeScript; optional L-BFGS in Rust
- **Stochastic**: Gillespie algorithm in Rust + WASM
- **Caching**: SQLite stores fitted parameters per dataset

-{"id":"phage_explorer-7gs","title":"Advanced: Functional Module Coherence & Stoichiometry Checker","description":"Segment genomes into functional modules (replication, morphogenesis, lysis). Evaluate stoichiometric balance (capsid:scaffold ratios). Flag incomplete or overrepresented modules, suggest missing partners. Module ribbon with green/amber/red indicators. Quick genome QC and design check. Novelty: Medium-high.","notes":"## FUNCTIONAL MODULE COHERENCE & STOICHIOMETRY CHECKER

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Segment genomes into functional modules (replication, morphogenesis, lysis, regulation) and evaluate stoichiometric balance (e.g., capsid:scaffold, tail fiber sets), flagging incomplete or overrepresented modules.

### How to Build

**Module Detection:**
- HMMER/domain co-occurrence graphs
- Rule-based module assignment:
  - Replication: polymerases, helicases, primases
  - Morphogenesis: capsid, portal, tail, baseplate
  - Lysis: holins, endolysins, spanins
  - Regulation: repressors, anti-repressors, anti-terminators

**Stoichiometry Rules:**
- Expected copy ratios from literature and structural data:
  - Major capsid : scaffold (typically 1:1)
  - Tail fiber sets (typically 6 or 12)
  - Portal : capsid (12:1 or 5:1)
- Compare to gene presence/duplication

**Coherence Score:**
- Penalize missing essential components
- Penalize excess paralogs
- Suggest likely missing partners from HMM profiles

### TUI Features
- Module ribbon with green/amber/red indicators
- Hover shows expected vs found counts
- 'Suggest' button highlights nearest homologs that could fill gaps
- Stoichiometry balance visualization
- Module-by-module breakdown panel

### Why It's Good
- Quick 'is this genome complete and balanced?' readout
- Great for QC and design validation
- Shows structural/assembly stoichiometry
- Explains why certain components must be balanced

### Implementation Stack
- HMMER precompute for domain detection
- TS scoring and rule evaluation
- Optional Rust+WASM for fast domain co-occurrence
- SQLite cache for module assignments
- Ink module ribbon UI

+{"id":"phage_explorer-7gs","title":"Advanced: Functional Module Coherence & Stoichiometry Checker","description":"Segment genomes into functional modules (replication, morphogenesis, lysis). Evaluate stoichiometric balance (capsid:scaffold ratios). Flag incomplete or overrepresented modules, suggest missing partners. Module ribbon with green/amber/red indicators. Quick genome QC and design check. Novelty: Medium-high.","notes":"## FUNCTIONAL MODULE COHERENCE & STOICHIOMETRY CHECKER

**Full TypeScript Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Perform comprehensive 'genome health check' by segmenting into functional modules and evaluating completeness, balance, and coherence.

### Mathematical Foundations

**Module Completeness Score:**
```
C_module = Σ(w_i × present_i) / Σ(w_i)

where:
- w_i: weight (1.0=critical, 0.5=common, 0.2=optional)
- present_i: 1 if gene found, 0 otherwise
```

**Stoichiometric Balance Score:**
```
imbalance_ij = |log2(observed_i / observed_j) - log2(r_ij)|
S_balance = 1 - tanh(Σ imbalance_ij / n_pairs)
```

**Module Coherence (Gene Neighborhood):**
```
Coherence = Σ(adjacent_same_module) / (Σ genes_in_module - 1)
```

**Expression Proxy from RBS:**
```
RBS_strength = PSSM_score(Shine-Dalgarno, -15 to -5)
Expected_copies ∝ RBS_strength × CAI
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `ModuleType`: replication, morphogenesis_head/tail, packaging, lysis, lysogeny, host_interaction, auxiliary
- `ModuleGene` with role, essentiality, expected copy number, RBS strength
- `FunctionalModule` with completeness, balance, coherence scores
- `StoichiometryRule` defining gene pair ratios
- `ModuleAnalysis` with quality grade A-F
- `MODULE_KEYWORDS` regex patterns per module type
- `ESSENTIAL_GENES` weighted lists per module
- `STOICHIOMETRY_RULES` (MCP:portal, terminase large:small, etc.)
- `estimateRBSStrength()` Shine-Dalgarno PSSM scoring
- `classifyGene()`, `determineEssentiality()`
- `assembleModules()` gene classification pipeline
- `computeCompletenessScore()`, `computeBalanceScore()`
- `computeCoherenceScore()` gene adjacency analysis
- `analyzeModuleCoherence()` main analysis function
- `renderModuleRibbon()`, `renderStoichiometryChart()`

### TUI Features
- Module Health Overview with progress bars per module
- Status indicators: ● complete, ◐ partial, ○ missing
- Missing essential gene warnings
- Stoichiometry panel per module showing:
  - Gene expression levels (bars)
  - Expected vs observed comparison
  - Balance score and coherence %
- Suggestions panel with priority levels
- Quality grade (A-F) prominently displayed
- [Tab] Next module, [S] Stoichiometry, [H] Find homologs

### Module Categories
- Replication: polymerase, helicase, primase, SSB
- Head: MCP, portal, scaffold, protease
- Tail: major tail, tape measure, fiber, baseplate
- Packaging: large/small terminase
- Lysis: endolysin, holin, spanin
- Lysogeny: integrase, CI, excisionase

 {"id":"phage_explorer-92s","title":"Foundation: Progressive Disclosure & User Journey","description":"## Progressive Disclosure & User Journey

Strategy for exposing features based on user expertise level.

### New User Journey (First 5 minutes)
- Only Layer 0 (12 sacred controls)
- Help overlay shows essential controls only
- Navigate phages, scroll sequences, toggle DNA/AA

### Intermediate User (First hour)
- Layer 0 + Layer 1 overlays
- Discover overlays via help
- Try diff mode, explore 3D model
- Help now shows 'Advanced: press A for more...'

### Power User
- Full Layer 0-4 access
- Uses command palette (: or Ctrl+P)
- Runs simulations, does comparative analysis
- Discovers features through exploration

### Key Insight
Every feature reachable in ≤3 keypresses but invisible until needed.

Depends on: Help System (phage_explorer-ofm), UI Design System (phage_explorer-dx3)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:35.931114-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ofm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:01.879645-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-dx3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:02.988977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-9jc","title":"Advanced: Cocktail Resistance Evolution Simulator","description":"Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using Gillespie/tau-leaping. Params from genome: receptor diversity, Sie genes, CRISPR spacer matches. Live ASCII trajectories, risk meter, side-by-side mono vs cocktail. Turns genomic evidence into dosing/risk guidance. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High (stochastic processes, evolutionary dynamics).","notes":"## COCKTAIL RESISTANCE EVOLUTION SIMULATOR

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using genome-derived parameters (receptor diversity, anti-defense, spacer proximity).

### How to Build

**Stochastic Engine:**
- Gillespie/tau-leaping in Rust+WASM (rand + rayon)
- Events: bacterial growth, phage adsorption, resistance mutation, receptor switching

**Genome-Derived Parameters:**
- Receptor diversity from tail fiber annotation
- Sie genes (superinfection exclusion)
- CRISPR spacer matches (immunity potential)
- Anti-defense gene count

**User Inputs:**
- MOI (multiplicity of infection)
- Dosing cadence (hours between doses)
- Population size (CFU/mL)
- Cocktail composition (1-5 phages)

**Outputs:**
- Probability of resistance over time
- Time-to-resistance distribution
- Compare mono vs cocktail curves
- Optimal cocktail selection

### TUI Features
- Live ASCII trajectories (multiple runs overlaid)
- Risk meter updates in real-time
- Side-by-side mono vs cocktail comparison
- Sliders: MOI, dose interval, population size
- Toggle 'optimize cocktail' to auto-pick best trio
- Resistance probability timeline
- [R]un new simulation, [C]ompare, [O]ptimize

### Why It's Good
- Turns genomic evidence into therapy guidance
- Quantifies resistance risk over time
- Compares mono vs cocktail robustness
- Identifies synergistic combinations

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM for Gillespie simulator (rand, rayon)
- TS/Ink UI for sliders and live trajectories
- SQLite cache for genome-derived parameters
- ASCII braille for trajectory plots
